headline: Trump’s DOJ withdrew subpoenas targeting Washington Post and WSJ reporters
body: The Washington Post and the Wall Street Journal waged a secret legal fight to stop the US government from subpoenaing several reporters in connection with national security leak probes, the Post reported Tuesday.

headline: Three remaining '60 Minutes' correspondents say they are staying
body: The three remaining “60 Minutes” correspondents told colleagues Friday that they will stay with the embattled newsmagazine

headline: Analysis: Why CBS News believes ‘60 Minutes’ needs a big disruption
body: CBS News is taking a huge risk by overhauling “60 Minutes.” Why blow up a profitable, prestigious newsmagazine when there is so much else at CBS that needs fixing?

headline: Nearly a third of Americans think the latest assassination attempt against Trump was staged
body: CNN’s Tom Foreman reports on the findings of a new Fox News poll

headline: Reporter details catastrophic humanitarian crisis unfolding amid aid cuts
body: New York Times reporter Peter Goodman joins The Lead.

headline: 5 things to know for May 18: Prayer event, Trump’s new warning, primaries, Ebola outbreak, severe storms
body: CNN’s 5 Things AM brings you the news you need to know every morning.

headline: New reporting on President Trump's push to sniff out sources of Iran stories
body: In a new escalation against press freedoms, officials tell CNN that President Trump has personally pushed the Department of Justice to subpoena reporters covering the Iran war in an effort to flush out their sources. CNN News Central’s Kate Bolduan discusses with CNN Chief Media Analyst Brian Stelter.

headline: Breaking down video of the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
body: CNN’s Josh Campbell breaks down newly released video of gunman Cole Tomas Allen in the lead-up to the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting.
